rationale: The cited paper is discussed explicitly in the hypothesis-development section, not just in a background list. The citing authors use Heilman et al. (2002) as empirical precedent for a psychological income or windfall effect, then map that logic to their price-war setting to motivate expected short-run spending increases. This gives the citation a clear functional role in conceptual framing and interpretation of mechanisms.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the windfall/psychological income insight from Heilman et al. (2002) to a new context: a large retail price war. It uses that prior result to motivate the hypothesis that sudden, highly publicized price reductions can temporarily raise spending per shopping trip.
